scope: Annual financial statements audit for FY2024 (year ended 31 March 2024)
executiveSummary: Qualified audit opinion issued. Material misstatements in revenue recognition and going concern disclosures. Irregular expenditure of R5.8bn identified.

findings.title: Revenue Recognition — Electricity Sales
findings.description: Unbilled electricity revenue of R12.4bn not consistently recognised in accordance with IFRS 15. Estimation methodology requires strengthening.
findings.recommendation: Implement robust IFRS 15 compliant revenue recognition policy with monthly reconciliation.
findings.managementResponse: Agreed. IFRS 15 implementation project underway with target completion Q2 FY2025.
findings.actionTaken: IFRS 15 policy drafted. Smart meter rollout to improve billing accuracy.

findings.title: Irregular Expenditure — Supply Chain Management
findings.description: Irregular expenditure of R5.8bn identified, primarily in emergency procurement and deviations from competitive bidding processes.
findings.recommendation: Implement consequence management, strengthen SCM controls, reduce emergency procurement.
findings.managementResponse: 23 disciplinary cases initiated. SCM reform programme approved by board.
findings.actionTaken: Consequence management committee established. 8 cases finalised.

findings.title: Going Concern Uncertainty
findings.description: Eskom's ability to continue as a going concern is dependent on continued government support and successful implementation of the debt relief package.
findings.recommendation: Disclose going concern uncertainty prominently. Develop credible path to financial sustainability.
findings.managementResponse: Going concern disclosed in financial statements. Turnaround plan approved by board.
findings.actionTaken: Turnaround plan implementation in progress. R254bn debt relief package being implemented.

scope: Annual financial statements audit for FY2024 (year ended 31 March 2024)
executiveSummary: Qualified audit opinion. Irregular expenditure of R4.2bn. Legacy State Capture procurement irregularities continue to impact financial statements.

findings.title: Legacy Irregular Expenditure — Locomotive Procurement
findings.description: R54bn irregular locomotive procurement contracts identified by Zondo Commission remain unresolved. Civil recovery actions in progress but outcome uncertain.
findings.recommendation: Pursue civil recovery, implement SIU recommendations, strengthen procurement governance.
findings.managementResponse: SIU civil recovery actions in progress. R2.1bn recovered to date. New procurement governance framework implemented.
findings.actionTaken: Civil recovery litigation ongoing. 15 disciplinary cases finalised.

findings.title: Debt Covenant Compliance
findings.description: Transnet is in breach of financial covenants on R28bn of debt facilities. Lenders have granted temporary waivers pending restructuring.
findings.recommendation: Negotiate permanent covenant amendments, improve financial performance, reduce debt.
findings.managementResponse: Covenant waiver obtained from 85% of lenders. Debt restructuring plan approved.

companyName: Passenger Rail Agency of South Africa
scope: Annual financial statements and performance audit for FY2024
executiveSummary: Adverse audit opinion issued for fifth consecutive year. Material misstatements in financial statements. Irregular expenditure exceeds R20bn cumulatively. Service delivery failures documented.

findings.title: Material Misstatements in Financial Statements
findings.description: Financial statements contain material misstatements in property, plant and equipment (R8.2bn), revenue (R1.4bn) and provisions (R2.1bn).
findings.recommendation: Appoint qualified CFO, strengthen finance team, implement financial management improvement plan.
findings.managementResponse: New CFO appointed. Financial management improvement plan approved.
findings.actionTaken: CFO appointed. Finance team capacity building underway.

findings.title: Rolling Stock Fleet Renewal Programme Irregularities
findings.description: R51bn Rolling Stock Fleet Renewal Programme has R12.4bn in irregular expenditure. Contract management weaknesses identified.
findings.recommendation: Implement SIU recommendations, strengthen contract management, pursue consequence management.
findings.managementResponse: SIU investigation ongoing. Contract management framework being strengthened.
findings.actionTaken: SIU investigation in progress. 8 disciplinary cases initiated.

companyName: Airports Company South Africa SOC Limited
scope: Annual financial statements audit for FY2024 (year ended 31 March 2024)
executiveSummary: Unqualified audit opinion. Strong financial performance with revenue exceeding target. No material findings.

findings.title: IT General Controls — Access Management
findings.description: Privileged access management controls for airport management systems require strengthening. Segregation of duties gaps identified in 3 systems.
findings.recommendation: Implement privileged access management solution, review user access rights quarterly.
findings.managementResponse: Agreed. PAM solution implemented. Quarterly access review process established.
findings.actionTaken: PAM solution deployed. Access rights reviewed and remediated.
